title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: Claims lack citation to controlling primary authorities (specific federal statutes/regulations like ECOA/Reg B, FCRA/Reg V, and key CFPB/FTC rules) and to pivotal case law—relying on unidentified source IDs is insufficient to assess legal weight.

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: The term “U.S. context” is too broad: the analysis needs jurisdictional granularity (federal vs state, and note circuit/supreme court splits or preemption issues) to be legally usable.

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: c001 is directionally correct but too general—cite specific primary authorities (e.g., ECOA/Reg B, FCRA, controlling statutes/regulations and leading cases) and analyze state-law variance and preemption rather than relying on opaque supporting_sources.

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: Assess the authoritative weight of cited agency materials—distinguish binding regulations and statutory text from nonbinding guidance, supervisory guidance, or consent orders when evaluating compliance of AI-generated explanations.
